issues.title: scaffold: pyproject, package skeleton, AGPL-3.0 license
issues.description: Create the Python package skeleton: pyproject.toml with build-system, project metadata (name=noesis-engine, python>=3.10, license=AGPL-3.0), optional extras [audio,openai,anthropic,local,dev]. Create empty noesis_engine/__init__.py exposing __version__. Add tests/ directory with __init__.py. Add LICENSE file (AGPL-3.0). No runtime logic yet.
issues.acceptance_criteria: pip install -e .[dev] succeeds. pytest runs (collects 0 tests). import noesis_engine works and exposes __version__. No dependency on Po_core anywhere.

issues.title: core: data types and exceptions
issues.description: Define dataclasses / Pydantic models for Utterance, DecomposedUtterance, ConflictCategory (Enum: ETHICAL, RESOURCE, TEMPORAL, VALUE, METHODOLOGICAL), AxisVector, SpeakerAnalysis, MinorityEvaluation, NoesisOutput. Also create noesis_engine/core/exceptions.py with NoesisError base plus PersonaNotFoundError, CategoryClassificationError, LLMBackendError.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Unit tests instantiate every type with valid and invalid payloads. mypy --strict passes on core/types.py. No external side effects.

issues.title: llm: abstract LLMClient base
issues.description: Define abstract LLMClient with chat(system_prompt, messages, schema=None, temperature=0.0) -> StructuredResponse. Define Message, StructuredResponse dataclasses. No concrete backend. Include deterministic FakeLLMClient for tests that returns canned JSON per prompt pattern.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Subclass FakeLLMClient usable in tests. Schema validation enforced when schema given. Raises LLMBackendError on malformed structured output.

issues.title: llm: Anthropic client
issues.description: Implement AnthropicClient(LLMClient) using the anthropic SDK. Respect schema argument via tool-use or response_format. Read API key from config / env. Guard import so the package still imports when anthropic is not installed (raise only on instantiation).
issues.acceptance_criteria: Integration test (skipped without ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) returns parsed JSON. Unit test with mocked SDK passes. Importing noesis_engine without anthropic installed still works.

issues.title: llm: OpenAI client
issues.description: Implement OpenAIClient(LLMClient) using the openai SDK with structured outputs (JSON schema). Same lazy-import guard pattern as T004.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Unit test with mocked SDK passes. Lazy import verified. Schema enforced.

issues.title: llm: local client stub
issues.description: Implement LocalLLMClient(LLMClient) stub that documents the adapter target (llama-cpp-python / vLLM). Raise NotImplementedError with a clear message on instantiation. Provide the method signatures so future swap-in is frictionless.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Instantiation raises NotImplementedError with guidance text. Class importable and registered in llm package.

issues.title: persona: Persona abstract base and prompt contract
issues.description: Define Persona ABC with attributes: id, display_name, philosophical_tradition, axes (list[str]). Method build_system_prompt() -> str. Method analyze(client: LLMClient, utterances: list[DecomposedUtterance]) -> list[AxisVector]. The base encodes the JSON output schema so every persona returns the same shape.
issues.acceptance_criteria: ABC enforces required fields. analyze() uses a shared JSON schema. Unit test uses FakeLLMClient and a trivial subclass.

issues.title: persona: Kantian
issues.description: Implement KantianPersona. Axes: duty, universalizability, autonomy, dignity, constraint_respect. System prompt grounds reasoning in the categorical imperative and treatment of persons as ends. No decorative philosophy: each axis must map to a concrete question the persona asks of an utterance.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Unit test with FakeLLMClient validates axis keys match the declared axes list. Prompt does not reference other personas.

issues.title: persona: Aristotelian
issues.description: Implement AristotelianPersona. Axes: virtue, excellence, telos, phronesis, mean. Prompt grounds reasoning in eudaimonia and character.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Axis keys stable across runs. Schema validation passes.

issues.title: persona: Cartesian
issues.description: Implement CartesianPersona. Axes: method, clarity, distinctness, systematic_doubt, decomposition. Prompt grounds reasoning in the method of Discourse on Method.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Stable output shape. Prompt is method-centric, not epistemology-centric.

issues.title: persona: Hegelian
issues.description: Implement HegelianPersona. Axes: development, contradiction, synthesis, historical_trajectory, possibility.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Axis keys stable. Prompt explicitly frames statements as moments in a dialectic.

issues.title: persona: Utilitarian
issues.description: Implement UtilitarianPersona. Axes: aggregate_utility, consequence_breadth, preference_satisfaction, scope_of_moral_patients, marginal_impact.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Stable output. Prompt avoids mixing with deontology.

issues.title: persona: Deontological
issues.description: Implement DeontologicalPersona. Axes: rule_adherence, rights_protection, prohibition_strength, role_obligation, procedural_justice. Distinct from Kantian: rule-centric rather than universalizability-centric.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Prompt explicitly contrasts rule-following with universalization so overlap with Kantian is minimized.

issues.title: persona: VirtueEthics
issues.description: Implement VirtueEthicsPersona. Axes: character_trait_weight, practical_wisdom, community_context, role_excellence, habit_formation. Distinct from Aristotelian: trait-focused rather than telos-focused.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Axis keys distinct from Aristotelian. Tests confirm no key overlap.

issues.title: persona: Existentialist
issues.description: Implement ExistentialistPersona. Axes: authenticity, freedom, responsibility, finitude, project_commitment.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Prompt anchors on authentic choice under finitude.

issues.title: persona: Pragmatist
issues.description: Implement PragmatistPersona. Axes: workability, experimental_attitude, consequence_in_practice, fallibilism, community_of_inquiry.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Prompt focuses on what difference the claim makes in practice.

issues.title: persona: registry
issues.description: Provide PERSONA_REGISTRY: dict[str, type[Persona]] mapping ids to classes. Expose get_persona(id) and list_personas(). Registry is the single source of truth for selector and API.
issues.acceptance_criteria: All nine personas registered. get_persona raises PersonaNotFoundError on unknown id. list_personas returns stable ordering.

issues.title: input: text loader
issues.description: Implement load_text(path_or_str, format='auto') -> list[Utterance]. Support JSON list of {speaker_id, text, timestamp?} and a simple 'Speaker: text' plain-text format.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Round-trip test for both formats. Malformed input raises ValueError with a line pointer.

issues.title: input: speaker separator (text mode)
issues.description: Given a list of Utterance, group by speaker_id preserving order. If speaker_id is missing or equals 'unknown', do not fabricate IDs; flag them for downstream handling.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Group ordering matches input order. Unknown speakers surface as a separate bucket with a warning.

issues.title: input: audio loader (Whisper, optional extra)
issues.description: Implement load_audio(path) -> list[Utterance]. Use openai-whisper or faster-whisper (behind the [audio] extra). Diarization via pyannote is out of scope for v1; use a naive segmenter and label speakers as 'S1','S2' in deterministic order.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Lazy import so core works without audio extra installed. Integration test uses a short bundled wav fixture.

issues.title: decomposition: utterance decomposer
issues.description: Implement decompose(utterances, client) -> list[DecomposedUtterance]. Uses the LLM with a fixed JSON schema producing claims, premises, implicit_values. Temperature 0.0.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Schema enforced. FakeLLMClient test exercises all fields. Deterministic given identical fake responses.

issues.title: classification: category classifier (Stage 1)
issues.description: Implement classify(decomposed, client) -> ConflictCategory. JSON-schema-constrained single-label output. On ambiguity, raises CategoryClassificationError rather than guessing.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Exactly one of the five categories returned. Invalid labels raise the dedicated exception.

issues.title: persona: selector (Stage 2)
issues.description: Implement select_personas(category) -> list[Persona]. Hard-coded allocation table: ETHICAL->[Kantian,Utilitarian,VirtueEthics]; RESOURCE->[Utilitarian,Deontological,VirtueEthics]; TEMPORAL->[Existentialist,Pragmatist,Hegelian]; VALUE->[Kantian,Aristotelian,Hegelian]; METHODOLOGICAL->[Cartesian,Pragmatist,Aristotelian]. No LLM call. No override hook.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Table is immutable (tuple). Tests assert exact persona ids per category. Attempting to mutate raises.

issues.title: analysis: axis analyzer
issues.description: Implement analyze_axes(decomposed, personas, client) -> list[AxisVector]. For every (persona, speaker) pair, call persona.analyze and collect vectors. Fail loud on schema violation.
issues.acceptance_criteria: N_speakers * N_personas vectors produced. Stable ordering. FakeLLMClient covers success and malformed output.

issues.title: analysis: vector math
issues.description: Pure functions: pairwise_diff(vectors) -> dict[(speaker_a,speaker_b), float], intersection_axes(vectors, threshold) -> list[str], cluster(vectors) -> list[list[str]]. Distance uses axis-aligned cosine over the union of axis keys (missing -> 0).
issues.acceptance_criteria: 100% unit-tested pure functions. No LLM, no IO. Deterministic.

issues.title: analysis: minority evaluator
issues.description: Given all utterances plus which were rejected (input-provided or inferred from clustering), ask the LLM per-persona to score philosophical_value in [0,1], rejection_reason_structural, preserved_insight. Never drops a rejected utterance.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Every rejected utterance produces a MinorityEvaluation. Values outside [0,1] rejected by schema.

issues.title: output: schemas and formatter
issues.description: Assemble NoesisOutput from pipeline stage results. Provide to_json(output), to_markdown(output). Markdown presents intersection points as suggestions, never as prescriptions.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Golden-file tests for both renderers. Markdown contains a non-coercion notice.

issues.title: config: settings and env loading
issues.description: Pydantic Settings (pydantic-settings) with fields: llm_backend ('anthropic'|'openai'|'local'|'fake'), api keys, default_temperature, audio_enabled. Loads .env if present.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Missing backend key surfaces a clear error only when a real backend is instantiated, not at import time.

issues.title: core: pipeline orchestrator
issues.description: Implement NoesisPipeline with run(utterances) -> NoesisOutput wiring: separator -> decomposer -> classifier -> selector -> axis analyzer -> vector math -> minority evaluator -> formatter. Single LLMClient injected at construction. No persona choice exposed.
issues.acceptance_criteria: End-to-end test with FakeLLMClient returns a NoesisOutput. Pipeline refuses to proceed on any stage failure rather than silently degrading.

issues.title: api: FastAPI server
issues.description: FastAPI app with POST /analyze/text, POST /analyze/audio (gated by audio extra), GET /personas, GET /categories, GET /healthz. Dependency-inject the NoesisPipeline. OpenAPI docs auto-generated.
issues.acceptance_criteria: TestClient integration test for each endpoint. Audio endpoint returns 503 when extra not installed.

issues.title: cli: entry point
issues.description: Provide 'noesis' console script via pyproject entry point: 'noesis analyze <path>' prints markdown output; '--json' switches format.
issues.acceptance_criteria: subprocess test exercises both output modes on a fixture.

issues.title: ci: GitHub Actions
issues.description: Workflow: matrix python 3.10/3.11/3.12, steps: install [dev], ruff check, mypy --strict noesis_engine, pytest -q. No network-dependent tests on CI.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Workflow green on a fresh clone. mypy strict passes. ruff clean.

issues.title: release: PyPI publish workflow
issues.description: Tag-triggered workflow using pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish with trusted publishing. Builds sdist + wheel.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Dry-run on a pre-release tag uploads to TestPyPI successfully.

issues.title: tests: integration scenarios
issues.description: Golden scenarios: budget meeting (4 speakers across four grammars), ethical dilemma, methodology dispute. Use FakeLLMClient with canned persona outputs to assert category, persona set, and minority evaluation presence.
issues.acceptance_criteria: All three scenarios produce the expected category and at least one MinorityEvaluation with non-empty preserved_insight.

issues.title: docs: persona catalog
issues.description: One page per persona: id, axes (name + one-sentence operational meaning), the question each persona asks of an utterance. No decorative philosophy. Every axis must be answerable from a text utterance.
issues.acceptance_criteria: Every persona in PERSONA_REGISTRY is documented. Axes in docs match code (validated by a docs test).
